marketFocus: Super Week Central Bank Sweep Delivered — BOJ Hawkish, ECB Hawkish, BOE 9-0 Hawkish, SNB Intervenes: All five G10 central banks (Fed, BOJ, ECB, BOE, SNB, Riksbank) held rates on Wednesday and Thursday. The common theme: hold until the economic impact of the Middle East energy shock becomes clearer. The ECB was the most hawkish — HICP 2026 upgraded to 2.6% (from 1.9%), GDP 2026 cut to 0.9% (from 1.2%); source reports say hike talk starts in April with a move more likely in June. Barclays and JPMorgan now forecast an ECB hike in April. The BOE delivered a 9-0 unanimous hold (vs 7-2 expected), removed language suggesting further cuts, and MPC member Mann signalled a potential hike. Goldman Sachs now expects the BOE on hold all 2026 with easing starting 2027. The BOJ's Ueda was hawkish — highlighted improving wage momentum at SMEs. The SNB held at 0.00% but issued explicit FX intervention language: 'willingness to intervene has increased'; CHF is 2.5% firmer on trade-weighted basis since December.

Oil Retreats on Netanyahu 'War Ending Sooner' Comments — WTI Settled $95.55/bbl, Brent $108.65/bbl: Oil partially retreated from Thursday's peak (Brent $119.50/bbl, WTI $107.50/bbl — the widest WTI/Brent spread since 2015 at ~$12/bbl) after Israeli PM Netanyahu said 'Iran has no capacity to enrich uranium or make ballistic missiles after 20 days of war' and that the war 'will end much sooner than people think.' However, Israel's Chief of Staff said they have not reached the 'halfway point' of operations and there is no timetable for the end. Saudi officials see the base case for oil rising to $180/bbl if disruptions persist until late April. QatarEnergy CEO confirmed 2 of 14 LNG trains and 1 of 2 gas-to-liquids facilities were damaged — may declare force majeure on long-term LNG contracts for up to five years. Kuwait suspended operations at Mina Abdullah and Mina Al-Ahmadi refineries after drone strikes.

Dollar Hit as Peers Hold Higher for Longer — DXY Nursing Losses, EUR/USD Briefly Above 1.1600, GBP/USD at 1.3321: The US FX Wrap headline was 'Dollar hit as peers also hold higher for longer; optimism surrounding Middle-East conflict from Netanyahu's pressers accelerates move.' DXY subdued around 100.00 (99.99-100.31 range). EUR/USD briefly returned above 1.1600 post-ECB before mildly pulling back. GBP/USD strengthened to the top of the 1.3245-1.3321 range post-BOE. USD/JPY slumped briefly below 158.00 on Ueda's hawkish tone before a mild recovery. UK Jobs data beat on employment (+84k vs -4k exp) but wages softened (Earnings excl. Bonus 3.8% vs 4.0% exp). German PPI Feb -0.5% MoM (vs +0.3% exp), -3.3% YoY (vs -2.7% exp) — deflation deepening. UK PSNB Feb 14.3B (vs 8.5B exp) — fiscal miss. CAD Retail Sales (Jan) today at 13:30 FFT is the only remaining high-impact event of the week.

USD.tone: Dollar Hit — DXY 100.00, Peers Hold Higher for Longer, No New Hawkish Catalyst
USD.summary: The dollar was hit on Thursday as all five G10 central banks held rates with a hawkish tilt, reinforcing the 'higher for longer' narrative for peers — not just the Fed. DXY was subdued around 100.00 within a 99.99-100.31 range. The FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% on Wednesday as expected, with Powell reiterating policy remains restrictive and no imminent cuts. The Fed's hawkish hold is now the baseline, not a differentiator — EUR, GBP, and JPY all moved hawkish to catch up. Netanyahu's 'war ending sooner' comments spurred risk appetite and accelerated USD selling. US Jobless Claims 205k (vs 215k exp) — a beat, but insufficient to reverse the dollar's slide. Philly Fed Manufacturing 18.1 (vs 16.3 prev) — solid. No major US data today. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: DXY subdued 99.99-100.31 — 'dollar hit as peers hold higher for longer' (US FX Wrap)
FOMC held 3.50-3.75% Wed; Powell: 'policy remains restrictive, no imminent cuts'
EUR, GBP, JPY all moved hawkish — Fed's hold no longer a USD differentiator
US Jobless Claims 205k (vs 215k exp) — beat, but insufficient to reverse USD slide
Netanyahu 'war ending sooner' comments spurred risk appetite, accelerated USD selling

EUR.tone: ECB Hawkish Hold — HICP 2026 Upgraded to 2.6%, Hike Talk Starting April
EUR.summary: EUR shifted to Bullish on Thursday after the ECB delivered a hawkish hold. HICP 2026 was upgraded to 2.6% (from 1.9%), GDP 2026 cut to 0.9% (from 1.2%). ECB source reports noted officials see the need for possible rate hike talk to start in April, with a move more likely in June. Barclays and JPMorgan now forecast an ECB hike in April. EUR/USD briefly returned above 1.1600 before mildly pulling back overnight. ECB's Nagel said the ECB would need a hike in April 'if the price outlook sours, will act with necessary resolve.' Multiple ECB speakers this morning (Nagel, Muller, Kazak) all confirming the hawkish tilt. German PPI Feb -0.5% MoM (vs +0.3% exp) — deflationary miss, but the energy shock inflation narrative dominates. Weekly Weak Bearish vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment (reversal).
drivers: ECB held — HICP 2026 upgraded to 2.6% (from 1.9%); GDP 2026 cut to 0.9% (from 1.2%)
ECB source reports: hike talk starts April, move more likely June; Barclays/JPMorgan forecast April hike
EUR/USD briefly above 1.1600 post-ECB; Nagel: 'would need hike in April if price outlook sours'
German PPI Feb -0.5% MoM (vs +0.3% exp), -3.3% YoY — deflationary miss but energy narrative dominates
ECB speakers today: Nagel (hawkish), Muller, Kazak — all confirming hawkish tilt

GBP.tone: BOE 9-0 Hawkish Hold — Removed Cut Language, Mann Signals Hike, UK Jobs +84k Beat
GBP.summary: GBP shifted to Bullish on Thursday after the BOE delivered a 9-0 unanimous hold (vs 7-2 expected) — the most hawkish outcome possible. The statement removed language suggesting further cuts and retained optionality if the shock proves short-lived. MPC member Mann signalled a potential hike. Governor Bailey cautioned against strong conclusions about rate hikes, but the overall tone implies easing is off the table near term. Goldman Sachs now expects the BOE on hold all 2026 with easing starting 2027. UK Jobs data was mixed but net positive: Employment Change +84k (vs -4k exp) — massive beat; Unemployment 5.2% (in line); Earnings excl. Bonus 3.8% (below 4.0% exp) — wages softening. GBP/USD traded towards the top of the 1.3245-1.3321 range. UK PSNB Feb 14.3B (vs 8.5B exp) — fiscal miss but not a GBP driver today. Weekly Weak Bearish vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment (reversal).
drivers: BOE held 9-0 (vs 7-2 exp) — removed cut language; Mann signals potential hike
Goldman Sachs: BOE on hold all 2026, easing starts 2027 at terminal 3.0%
UK Employment Change +84k (vs -4k exp) — massive beat; Unemployment 5.2% in line
UK Earnings excl. Bonus 3.8% (vs 4.0% exp) — wages softening, stagflation risk remains
GBP/USD at top of 1.3245-1.3321 range; UK ministers visiting EU capitals next week for financial services ties

JPY.tone: Ueda Hawkish — Wage Momentum Improving, USD/JPY Slumped Below 158.00
JPY.summary: JPY shifted to Bullish on Thursday as BOJ Governor Ueda delivered a hawkish tone, highlighting improving wage momentum at small and medium-sized firms and signalling the BOJ will monitor energy/FX market developments. USD/JPY slumped briefly below 158.00 before a mild recovery facilitated by dollar composure, with further momentum contained by the absence of Japanese participants (Vernal Equinox holiday). Haven flows resumed with the cover of a hawkish Ueda, even as gold was not sought amid heavy intraday losses. Japan's Vernal Equinox holiday means thin liquidity today. The structural bearish case (90%+ crude imports from Middle East) remains, but the near-term momentum has shifted to JPY Bullish on the Ueda hawkish surprise. Weekly Bearish vs Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment (reversal).
drivers: BOJ held 0.75% — Ueda hawkish: 'prelim data shows wage momentum at SMEs could be better than past years'
USD/JPY slumped briefly below 158.00 on Ueda's hawkish tone + haven flows
Japan Vernal Equinox holiday — thin liquidity, cash treasuries trade shut overnight
USD/JPY mild recovery as dollar regained composure; further upside capped by holiday absence
Structural bearish case (90%+ crude imports) remains but near-term momentum shifted bullish on Ueda

AUD.tone: AUD/USD Declined — Gold/Copper Losses, Global CB Hawkish Tilt, ASX 200 Dragged Lower
AUD.summary: AUD shifted to Bearish on Thursday as AUD/USD declined following sharp losses in gold (below $4,600/oz, testing $4,500/oz) and copper (briefly below $12,000/t, erasing all 2026 gains). Global central banks skewed more hawkish against the backdrop of the Iranian war-driven energy shock, weighing on growth-sensitive assets. ASX 200 was dragged lower by weakness in materials and commodity-related sectors, with losses cushioned by strength in telecoms and defensives. The post-RBA hike tailwind has faded as the global risk-off from the central bank hawkish sweep dominates. Australia Employment data (Thu 01:30 FFT) has already passed — the result was not mentioned in the reports, suggesting it was in line or a non-event. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment (reversal).
drivers: AUD/USD declined — gold broke below $4,600/oz to test $4,500/oz; copper briefly below $12,000/t
ASX 200 dragged lower by materials/commodity weakness; telecoms and defensives cushioned losses
Global CB hawkish sweep (BOJ, ECB, BOE, SNB) weighing on growth-sensitive assets
Post-RBA hike tailwind faded — global risk-off from central bank hawkish tilt dominates
Antipodeans saw mixed and rangebound trade amid cautious risk appetite

CAD.tone: WTI $95.55/bbl, Brent $108.65/bbl — Petro-Currency Bid Intact, Retail Sales Today
CAD.summary: CAD remains Bullish as oil prices, while retreating from Thursday's peak (Brent $119.50/bbl), are still elevated at WTI $95.55/bbl and Brent $108.65/bbl. The petro-currency bid is intact. Saudi officials see the base case for oil rising to $180/bbl if disruptions persist until late April. QatarEnergy confirmed 2 of 14 LNG trains damaged — potential force majeure for up to 5 years on LNG contracts. Kuwait suspended two refineries after drone strikes. The BOC decision on Wednesday was a hold, but the energy windfall continues to support CAD fundamentally. Canadian Retail Sales (Jan) at 13:30 FFT today is the only remaining high-impact event of the week — a beat would reinforce the CAD Bullish case. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI settled $95.55/bbl, Brent $108.65/bbl — petro-currency bid intact despite partial retreat
Saudi officials: base case for oil to rise to $180/bbl if disruptions persist until late April
QatarEnergy: 2 of 14 LNG trains damaged — potential force majeure up to 5 years
Kuwait suspended Mina Abdullah + Mina Al-Ahmadi refineries after drone strikes
CAD Retail Sales m/m (Jan) at 13:30 FFT today = only remaining high-impact event of the week

CHF.tone: SNB Explicit FX Intervention — 'Willingness to Intervene Has Increased', CHF Weakened Post-SNB
CHF.summary: CHF shifted to Bearish on Thursday after the SNB held at 0.00% but issued explicit FX intervention language. SNB Chairman Schlegel stated 'willingness to intervene in the foreign exchange market has increased' and that CHF is 2.5% firmer on trade-weighted basis since December. The SNB explicitly stated it 'counters a rapid and excessive appreciation of the CHF, which would jeopardize domestic price stability.' EUR/CHF rose towards 0.9100 as initial CHF weakness pared — the intervention stance was largely expected. The SNB's explicit intervention threat caps CHF upside and makes CHF a sell-on-rallies currency near term. Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: SNB held 0.00% — Schlegel: 'willingness to intervene has increased'; CHF 2.5% firmer trade-weighted since Dec
EUR/CHF rose towards 0.9100 post-SNB — initial CHF weakness pared as intervention stance was expected
SNB: 'counters a rapid and excessive appreciation of CHF, which would jeopardize price stability'
CHF sought as haven during uncertainty — but SNB intervention threat caps upside
Medium-term inflationary pressure 'virtually unchanged' since last assessment — no urgency to hike

NZD.tone: NZD GDP Q4 Weak — Antipodeans Mixed/Rangebound, No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD remains Bearish as the currency continues to underperform amid the risk-off environment and absence of domestic catalysts. NZD/USD shrugged off the weak GDP print (Q4 result released Wednesday night) as the move was already priced in following the Current Account miss. Antipodeans saw mixed and rangebound trade amid cautious risk appetite and the absence of tier-1 data. AUD continues to outperform NZD on the RBA hike differential. No RBNZ meeting until April. The NZD is the weakest of the Antipodeans and remains a sell-on-rallies currency. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: NZD/USD rangebound — shrugged off weak GDP Q4 as move was already priced post-Current Account miss
Antipodeans mixed/rangebound amid cautious risk appetite and absence of tier-1 data
AUD outperforming NZD on RBA hike differential — AUD/NZD divergence widening
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent
NZD weakest Antipodean — sell-on-rallies bias; no domestic catalyst to reverse
